utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan: health_coverage is present with plan names and costs (monthly and annual), but lacks explicit mention of mental health coverage details; still, it is a health insurance entry with costs. property_coverage is marked 'Pending verification' and has no costs, so it is not fully specified. travel_coverage is present with some features but no costs, so not fully specified. additional_services are listed but have no costs, so not fully specified. total_budget is present with monthly and annual totals, which are costs. Therefore, fully specified according to the format’s requirement of including costs: health_coverage (has costs) and total_budget (has costs) qualify; property_coverage lacks costs, travel_coverage lacks costs, additional_services lacks costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan mentions three specific insurance plan names: PrimeTravel Premium Plan (as the health primary_plan and also clearly listed under travel_insurance), EliteCare Premium Plan (as Raj's individual plan), and VitalPlus Standard Plan (as Aryan's individual plan). Property coverage is pending and other listed services are not tied to rated items. Using the ground-truth ratings: PrimeTravel Premium Plan under travel_insurance has rating 10; EliteCare Premium Plan under primary_health_coverage physical_health has rating 10; VitalPlus Standard Plan under primary_health_coverage physical_health has rating 6. There is no 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' entry in health physical/mental categories, so we do not assign a health rating to it—only the travel rating applies.
